allocations.rationale: Focuses on large and midcap stocks with strong 3-year returns (26.9%) and low fees (0.10%).

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Large & Mid Cap Fund

allocations.rationale: Balanced growth with consistent 3-year returns (21.1%) and low volatility.
Aggressive hybrid for tax efficiency (equity-taxed) with 3-year returns (18.7%).
Slab-taxed but better than debt funds (post-tax return ~5.25% vs 3.85% for liquid funds).
Emergency fund for liquidity, slab-taxed but low risk.

plan.setup_phase: Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on Groww or Kuvera. Step 2: Start SIPs in the above funds. Ensure you invest in the growth option for long-term gains.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Confirm all SIPs are active.
After 1 year: Check if any fund underperforms its category (e.g., Motilal Oswal vs ICICI Prudential).
After 3 years: Rebalance if any fund’s allocation deviates by >10%.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, check if equity allocation has grown beyond 60% (due to market gains). Move 5% to arbitrage or liquid funds to maintain balance.
plan.rationale: At 42, you can afford moderate risk for long-term goals. Equity funds (Large & Mid Cap, Aggressive Hybrid) will grow your corpus faster than debt. Arbitrage funds add stability without sacrificing tax efficiency. The liquid fund covers emergencies. This mix balances growth and safety for retirement and education.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year (e.g., 2008 or 2022). Your portfolio value will fall temporarily, but it will recover over time. Diversification across fund houses (Motilal Oswal, ICICI Prudential, WhiteOak) reduces risk.
plan.disclaimer: For research/educational purposes only. Not certified financial advice.

The long-term horizon (retirement, education) aligns with a 0.8 score. The equity funds have no beta/alpha data, but the arbitrage and liquid funds are passive, leading to a 0.2 portfolio activeness score.
